source: Deep & Cross Network for Ad Click Predictions.pdf

concepts.finding:
Te main contributions of the paper include:
• We propose a novel cross network that explicitly applies feature
crossing at each layer, eﬃciently learns predictive cross features
of boundeddegrees, and requires no manual feature engineering
or exhaustive searching.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

concepts.finding:
1
INTRODUCTION
Click-through rate (CTR) prediction is a large-scale problem that is
essential to multi-billion dollar online advertising industry.
concepts.implication: Directly applicable to CTR/CVR prediction in advertising systems

concepts.finding:
1.2
Main Contributions
In this paper, we propose the Deep & Cross Network (DCN) model
that enables Web-scale automaticfeature learning with both sparse
and dense inputs.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
In this paper, we pro-
pose the Deep & Cross Network (DCN) which keeps the beneﬁts of
a DNN model, and beyond that, it introduces a novel cross network
that is more eﬃcient in learning certain bounded-degree feature
interactions.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
Jointly training the cross and DNN compo-
nents together, however, eﬃciently captures predictive feature in-
teractions, and delivers state-of-the-art performance on the Criteo
CTR dataset.

nents together, however, eﬃciently captures predictive feature in-

concepts.finding:
1.1
Related Work
Due to the dramatic increase in size and dimensionality of datasets,
a number of methods have been proposed to avoid extensive task-
speciﬁc feature engineering, mostly based on embedding techniques
and neural networks.
concepts.implication: Useful for building item/user embeddings in retrieval systems

concepts.finding:
Tere has been research
[16, 17] showing that DNNs are able to approximate an arbitrary
function under certain smoothness assumptions to an arbitrary
accuracy, given suﬃciently many hidden units or hidden layers.
concepts.implication: Inform search ranking and retrieval optimization

hinges on a proper choice of cross features, an exponential problem
for which there is yet no clear eﬃcient method.

concepts.finding:
2.2
Cross Network
Te key idea of our novel cross network is to apply explicit feature
crossing in an eﬃcient way.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

concepts.finding:
Terefore, a cross network introduces negligible
complexity compared to its deep counterpart, keeping the overall
complexity for DCN at the same level as that of a traditional DNN.
concepts.implication: General ML methodology applicable to content recommendation

we introduce a deep network in parallel.

In the
large-parameter regime, the DNN closes some of the gap; however,
DCN still outperforms DNN by a large amount, suggesting that it
can eﬃciently learn some types of meaningful feature interactions
that even a huge DNN model cannot.
